page: Amazon Web Services u7in-24tb.224xlarge is an ultra-high-memory server instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features dedicated Intel x86_64 processors running at a clock speed of 1.9 GHz, delivering 896 virtual CPUs across 448 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled at 2.0 threads per core. The system is equipped with 24,576.0 GB of system memory, yielding approximately 27.43 GB of RAM per physical core.

This instance does not include local instance storage, requiring reliance on Amazon Elastic Block Store for persistent storage. It is network and EBS optimized, providing a baseline network bandwidth of 100 Gbps. The server does not feature any graphics processing units and includes zero complimentary public IPv4 addresses. The lack of local storage and the lower CPU clock speed of 1.9 GHz represent trade-offs for workloads requiring high single-core frequency or local NVMe scratch space.

Positioned in an ultra-high-end hardware class, the u7in-24tb.224xlarge offers massive resource density for enterprise-scale applications. It is designed qualitatively for workloads that require massive memory footprints, such as large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and massive-scale data analytics.
description: Amazon Web Services u7in-24tb.224xlarge is an ultra-high-memory instance designed for massive enterprise workloads. Running on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it features dedicated Intel x86_64 processors with 896 vCPUs operating at 1.9 GHz. The server is equipped with 24,576.0 GB of RAM, providing 27.43 GB per core, but lacks local storage, relying instead on its EBS-optimized architecture and 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. With no onboard GPUs, this instance represents a highly specialized hardware class optimized for extreme memory density. The trade-off of a lower 1.9 GHz CPU clock speed and the absence of local NVMe storage is balanced by its massive memory capacity. It is designed for large-scale in-memory databases, enterprise resource planning systems, and heavy data analytics pipelines requiring vast memory footprints.
og_description: Amazon Web Services u7in-24tb.224xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 896 vCPUs, 24,576 GB of RAM, 100 Gbps network bandwidth, and no local storage, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services u7in-24tb.224xlarge is a Nitro-based instance offering 896 vCPUs, 24 TB of RAM, and 100 Gbps network bandwidth.
tagline: An ultra-high-density memory instance featuring twenty-four terabytes of RAM and dedicated processors for massive enterprise in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel x86_64 architecture with 896 vCPUs and 448 physical cores.
100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with EBS optimization.
Zero local storage and zero onboard GPUs.
Powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor.
Designed for large-scale in-memory databases and enterprise applications.
